title: Fermented Dense Russian Flour Blini
description: These dense, chewy blini are made with wholemeal flour and fermented with a kimchi-based mixture that adds a tangy, slightly sour flavor. They are perfect for a hearty brunch or breakfast meal.

ingredients.name: wholemeal flour
ingredients.notes: use a strong wholemeal flour with a high protein content

ingredients.name: kimchi
ingredients.notes: use a spicy kimchi for extra flavor

ingredients.name: yeast
ingredients.notes: active dry yeast is best for fermentation

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use a flaky sea salt for the best flavor

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.notes: a little sugar helps the fermentation process

ingredients.name: eggs
ingredients.notes: use large eggs for the best texture

ingredients.name: melted butter
ingredients.notes: use high-quality, salted butter for the best flavor

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the wholemeal flour, yeast, and salt. Whisk to combine until the yeast is evenly distributed.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the water, kimchi, sugar, and melted butter.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it ferment in a warm place for 1-2 hours, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium-high heat.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Cook the blini for 1-2 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the blini warm with your favorite toppings, such as sour cream, jam, or caviar.
